# LinkedIn Profile Exporter

<img src="assets/icon/linkedin-profile-exporter-icon.png" alt="LinkedIn Profile Exporter icon" width="96" height="96" />

Contract-first browser extension and bookmarklet suite for exporting accessible LinkedIn
profile data to structured local files.

The v0.1.0 release is governed by OpenSpec in
`openspec/changes/ship-linkedin-exporter-v0-1-0/`. The implementation is local-explicit:
settings and extracted data stay in the browser by default, no analytics or remote
service is used, and tests run against deterministic fixtures rather than live LinkedIn
accounts.

## Targets

- WXT extension for Chrome/Chromium, Edge, Firefox, Safari, and documented mobile
  packaging paths.
- Bookmarklet exporter for low-friction local page export where browser/content-security
  policy allows it.
- Canonical profile schema with LinkedIn-specific sections such as courses, featured
  items, organizations, interests, test scores, and patents, plus JSON, JSON Resume,
  YAML Resume, CSV, XLSX, XML, and compact LLM Markdown exporters. Provenance,
  confidence, and diagnostics are available through the opt-in Include all fields
  setting.
- Fumadocs product documentation, generated assets, web-store materials, CI, hooks, and
  Justfile validation.

## What Gets Exported

The canonical output model is `linkedin-profile-exporter.profile.v1`. It preserves
LinkedIn-specific profile sections directly instead of forcing everything through a
generic resume projection:

| Section         | Captured fields                                                                                                               |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Identity        | Name, headline, location, industry, connections, followers, member URN, profile URL, about text, links, and imagery metadata. |
| Work            | Company, title, employment type, location, dates, description, company URL/logo/industry, and grouped nested roles.           |
| Education       | School, degree, field, dates, activities, description, school URL, and school logo.                                           |
| Credentials     | Skills with endorsements, licenses/certifications with issuer and credential metadata, courses, languages, and test scores.   |
| Portfolio       | Projects, publications, featured items, patents, honors/awards, volunteering, organizations, interests, and recommendations.  |
| Export metadata | Capture timestamp, source URL, locale, generator, selected formats, and filename template.                                    |

Clean exports omit provenance, confidence, and diagnostics by default. Turn on **Include
all fields** in settings when you need field provenance, confidence scores, Voyager
inventory diagnostics, and other troubleshooting metadata.

## Local Commands

Install Node `>=22.12.0` and pnpm. The official pnpm installation guide is:
<https://pnpm.io/installation>.

After the workspace is installed:

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check:profile-output
pnpm check:profile-output /path/to/profile-output.json
just quick
just ci
pnpm test:e2e
```

The profile-output checker validates the canonical model, default metadata stripping,
opt-in Include all fields behavior, and every export format. When a path is supplied,
the command prints only aggregate counts, diagnostic codes, and export byte sizes.

Package GitHub Release assets after a clean release gate:

```bash
mise exec node@24 -- pnpm release:github:package
```

The package command creates a credential-free draft-release packet under
`.release/github/v0.1.0/` with browser extension ZIPs, bookmarklet assets, Firefox
source-review material, checksums, release notes, and a generated
`gh release create --draft --verify-tag` command.

Use `just openspec` after public behavior, docs-generation, file-structure,
asset-format, validation, or agent-instruction changes.

## Browser Install Paths

Build target-specific extension output, then load the generated directory into the
browser for local testing.

| Browser         | Build command                                                      | Local install path                                                                                                                         |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Chrome/Chromium | `pnpm --filter @linkedin-profile-exporter/extension build:chrome`  | Open `chrome://extensions`, enable Developer Mode, choose Load unpacked, and select `apps/extension/.output/chrome-mv3`.                   |
| Microsoft Edge  | `pnpm --filter @linkedin-profile-exporter/extension build:edge`    | Open `edge://extensions`, enable Developer Mode, choose Load unpacked, and select `apps/extension/.output/edge-mv3`.                       |
| Firefox         | `pnpm --filter @linkedin-profile-exporter/extension build:firefox` | Open `about:debugging#/runtime/this-firefox`, choose Load Temporary Add-on, and select `apps/extension/.output/firefox-mv2/manifest.json`. |
| Safari          | `pnpm --filter @linkedin-profile-exporter/extension build:safari`  | Use the WXT Safari output with Apple’s Safari Web Extension packaging flow in Xcode before device/store testing.                           |

Official browser install references:
[Chrome load unpacked](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/get-started/tutorial/hello-world),
[Microsoft Edge sideloading](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/extensions/getting-started/extension-sideloading),
[Firefox temporary installation](https://extensionworkshop.com/documentation/develop/temporary-installation-in-firefox/),
and
[Safari Web Extension packaging](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/safariservices/packaging-a-web-extension-for-safari).

For Playwright E2E on a fresh machine, install the Chromium browser runtime first:

```bash
pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
pnpm test:e2e
```
